name:node_name: # ^ SkillTree demonstrates the graph_node family from Phase A1-A5 of graph_types.md.
# ^     - refs-exist validator (every name in graphParents/graphChildren must be a row in this file)
# ^     - acyclic validator (no cycles via graphChildren — this is a DAG, not a cycle graph)
# ^   See files.tsv for the edge file (SkillEdges.tsv) that carries per-edge data
# ^   like required parent levels — that's the graph_edge family from the same plan.
# ^ Author contract: declare prerequisites on the *graphParents* side only;
# ^ the engine fills in graphChildren automatically. (Per Open Question 5, the
# ^ derived back-references are NOT written back to the file on reformat.)
# ^ Multi-parent skills (tracking, huntersMark) demonstrate why a DAG — not a
# ^ tree — is the right shape here: a skill can have several prerequisites.

description:text: Notice subtle details, tracks, and hidden things
Strike from concealment for bonus damage
Follow a quarry through the wilderness
Mark a target so it can never escape your senses
